document.name: gan-harness-cli-showcase
document.title: GAN harness (CLI): Planner → Generator↔Evaluator contract negotiation → contract-graded build loop → PR
document.summary: Anthropic GAN-style long-running harness. A Planner writes a HIGH-LEVEL SPEC.md (no granular criteria); the Generator and a skeptical Evaluator then NEGOTIATE a rigid, browser-testable acceptance contract (contract.json, each criterion passes:false) before any code. The build loop generates, passes a deterministic build gate, and the Evaluator grades the running app against EACH contract criterion via Playwright MCP, updating passes flags; a progress.json agentic-memory log is read+appended every turn. Exits only when the build passes AND every contract criterion passes; opens a PR. interactive-cli family; GITHUB_TOKEN auto-injected.

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Redesign the Nimbus Notes landing page (src/routes/+page.svelte plus a global stylesheet) into a polished, DISTINCTIVE product landing page with a coherent visual identity — strong typography hierarchy, deliberate color/spacing, a hero, the three feature cards, a pricing section, and a clear primary CTA. Plain SvelteKit + CSS only; keep `npm run build` passing; do NOT change package.json deps or routing.
intent.description: What the agents change in the repo.

planAgent.title: Planner agent
planAgent.description: PLAN phase agent (clones + writes SPEC). Same workspace-backend family.

criticAgent.title: Critic agent
criticAgent.description: EVALUATOR agent — negotiates the contract AND grades the build (drives Playwright MCP).

intent.title: Redesign request
intent.default: Redesign the Nimbus Notes landing page (src/routes/+page.svelte plus a global stylesheet) into a polished, DISTINCTIVE product landing page with a coherent visual identity — strong typography hierarchy, deliberate color/spacing, a hero, the three feature cards, a pricing section, and a clear primary CTA. Plain SvelteKit + CSS only; keep `npm run build` passing; do NOT change package.json deps or routing.
intent.description: What the agents change in the repo.

planAgent.title: Planner agent
planAgent.description: PLAN phase agent (clones + writes SPEC). Same workspace-backend family.

criticAgent.title: Critic agent
criticAgent.description: EVALUATOR agent — negotiates the contract AND grades the build (drives Playwright MCP).

agentConfig.name: Planner
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the PLANNER for a coding redesign on a SvelteKit repo. FIRST clone the repo into /sandbox/work/repo by running EXACTLY this shell:
  cd /sandbox/work && rm -rf repo && git clone https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git repo && cd repo && git config user.name agent && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git remote set-url origin https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git
Then READ the repo (src/routes/+page.svelte, package.json) and write a HIGH-LEVEL product + design spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo). Keep it to product context, target audience, the desired mood/identity, and the FOUR design dimensions: design_quality, originality, craft, functionality. Deliberately stay HIGH-LEVEL: do NOT enumerate granular, implementation-level acceptance criteria — the Generator and the Evaluator negotiate the specific, testable criteria in the next phase. End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Redesign request:
" + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Repo to redesign: https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo
FIRST clone it into /sandbox/work/repo, authenticating with the GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable, by running this shell:
  cd /sandbox/work && rm -rf repo && git clone https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git repo && cd repo && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git config user.name agent && git remote set-url origin https://github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git
Then READ the repo and write a HIGH-LEVEL product+design spec to /sandbox/work/SPEC.md (product context, mood/identity, the four design dimensions). Do NOT enumerate granular acceptance criteria (those are negotiated next). Do NOT edit the repo. End with a one-line summary." }

with.command: cd /sandbox/work/repo && BASE=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo unknown); printf '{"baseline":"%s","log":[]}' "$BASE" > /sandbox/work/progress.json; cat /sandbox/work/progress.json

agentConfig.name: Proposer (Generator)
agentConfig.instructions: You are the GENERATOR in a contract NEGOTIATION, BEFORE writing any code. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Propose a concrete plan: break the redesign into a SMALL set of discrete, INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIABLE features. For EACH feature state (a) what you will implement, (b) EXACTLY how it can be verified in a browser (what to look for / interact with), and (c) a crisp acceptance criterion. On later rounds, READ /sandbox/work/contract-review.md (the Evaluator's objections) and REVISE your proposal to address every objection. Write your proposal to /sandbox/work/proposal.md (ONLY that file; do NOT edit the repo and do NOT write contract.json). End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Contract negotiation (proposal). Intent: " + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "\n\nRead /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and, if it exists, /sandbox/work/contract-review.md (the Evaluator's objections from the previous round). Write/revise /sandbox/work/proposal.md with discrete, browser-verifiable features + acceptance criteria per your instructions." }

agentConfig.name: Evaluator (Contract negotiation)
agentConfig.instructions: You are a HIGHLY SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR negotiating the acceptance contract for a redesign, BEFORE any code is written. You do NOT write code and you do NOT open a browser yet. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and /sandbox/work/proposal.md. Adversarially review the proposed features + acceptance criteria. DEFAULT TO REJECT. Reject criteria that are vague, unmeasurable, overly broad, or that omit important edge cases (empty/error states, responsive/mobile layout, keyboard focus / accessibility, interactive elements actually working). Push for every criterion to be SPECIFIC and BROWSER-VERIFIABLE. Write your objections + required changes to /sandbox/work/contract-review.md. THEN write the current negotiated contract to /sandbox/work/contract.json as STRICT JSON with EXACTLY this shape: {"agreed": <boolean>, "criteria": [{"id": "c1", "dimension": "design_quality|originality|craft|functionality", "description": "...", "verify": "how to check it in the browser", "passes": false}, ...]}. Every criterion MUST start with "passes": false. Set "agreed": true ONLY when EVERY criterion is specific, testable in a browser, and the set covers the key edge cases — otherwise "agreed": false and keep iterating. Aim for a TIGHT but COMPLETE set (typically 6-15 criteria). End with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Negotiate the acceptance contract. Read /sandbox/work/SPEC.md and /sandbox/work/proposal.md, write your objections to /sandbox/work/contract-review.md, and write/update /sandbox/work/contract.json (agreed + criteria, each passes:false) as STRICT JSON per your instructions. Be skeptical: set agreed:true ONLY when every criterion is specific, browser-verifiable, and covers edge cases." }

with.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do [ -s /sandbox/work/contract.json ] && break; sleep 1; done
python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json
try:
    d=json.load(open('/sandbox/work/contract.json'))
except Exception:
    print(json.dumps({'agreed':False,'criteriaCount':0,'error':'contract.json missing/invalid'}));raise SystemExit
crit=d.get('criteria') or []
print(json.dumps({'agreed':bool(d.get('agreed')),'criteriaCount':len(crit)}))
PYZZ

refine.while: ${ ( ( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) ) and ( (( (((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) as $v | ( ($v.meets_criteria) // (($v.overall_verdict // $v.verdict // "") | ascii_downcase | test("pass|approv|meet|satisf|accept")) // (($v.score // 0) >= 80) ) )) == true ) ) | not }

agentConfig.name: Generator
agentConfig.instructions: You are the GENERATOR. Implement the redesign in /sandbox/work/repo to satisfy the NEGOTIATED CONTRACT. GETTING UP TO SPEED (do this FIRST, every turn): read /sandbox/work/progress.json (the chronological log of prior iterations) and run `git -C /sandbox/work/repo log --oneline -10`. Then read /sandbox/work/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE acceptance criteria — each has id/description/verify/passes) and /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Focus on the criteria whose "passes" is false. Edit src/routes/+page.svelte and a global stylesheet (src/routes/+layout.svelte importing app.css, or a <style> block) — plain SvelteKit + CSS, NO new dependencies, do NOT touch package.json or routing. You do NOT grade your own work: a deterministic build gate + an independent skeptical Evaluator (who VIEWS the rendered page and tests EACH contract criterion) evaluate every attempt. Each turn: address the build output AND the Evaluator's per-criterion feedback, make the most impactful improvement toward the FAILING criteria, ensure `npm run build` passes, end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt:
${ "Redesign request: " + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the landing page") + "

Repo: /sandbox/work/repo. Authoritative acceptance criteria: /sandbox/work/contract.json. Spec: /sandbox/work/SPEC.md. Memory: /sandbox/work/progress.json (read it + git log FIRST)." + "

Your previous attempt:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(first attempt)") + "

Build gate (must end OBJECTIVE PASS):
" + ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") // "(not run yet)") + "

Evaluator per-criterion feedback (from the browser):
" + (try ((((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) | (.feedback // .summary // "(none)")) catch "(none)") + "

Make the build pass AND satisfy every FAILING contract criterion, then end with a brief summary." }

with.command: cd /sandbox/work/repo && (npm install --no-audit --no-fund >/tmp/install.log 2>&1 && git checkout -- package-lock.json 2>/dev/null; true && npm run build >/tmp/build.log 2>&1 && echo 'OBJECTIVE PASS: build OK' || { echo 'OBJECTIVE FAIL: build failed'; tail -40 /tmp/build.log; }) 2>&1; echo "__EXIT__$?"

agentConfig.name: Critic
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting, SKEPTICAL EVALUATOR with strong taste, independent of the builder; you do NOT edit the repo. You negotiated the contract; now GRADE the running app against it.

STEP 1 — start the app. The build already ran (node_modules + build output exist in /sandbox/work/repo). Start the preview server on port 4173 in the background and wait until it answers, by running this shell:
  cd /sandbox/work/repo && (nohup npm run preview -- --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) ; for i in $(seq 1 40); do curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4173/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done ; tail -5 /tmp/preview.log
If preview never answers, fall back to dev on the same port: (nohup npm run dev -- --port 4173 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) and wait the same way. Confirm the URL in /tmp/preview.log before continuing.

STEP 2 — inspect with the Playwright MCP browser tools (MCP server name 'playwright'): browser_navigate to http://127.0.0.1:4173/ , then browser_snapshot (accessibility tree) and browser_take_screenshot (filename 'critic-shot.png' → /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png). Resize the viewport (desktop/tablet/mobile) and browser_click the nav / primary CTA, re-snapshotting, to test interactivity + responsiveness. Use the BROWSER (Playwright MCP) — do not write your own screenshot scripts.

IMPORTANT — build artifacts are EXPECTED and NOT out-of-scope changes: IGNORE churn in package-lock.json, node_modules/, .svelte-kit/, and build/. Judge ONLY the redesign source (src/) and the rendered UI.

STEP 3 — read /sandbox/work/contract.json (the AUTHORITATIVE acceptance criteria), /sandbox/work/SPEC.md, and the source (src/routes/+page.svelte, +layout.svelte, any CSS).

STEP 4 — grade the rendered app against EACH criterion in contract.json. For each, decide passes true/false based on what you ACTUALLY SAW / INTERACTED WITH in the browser (use the criterion's "verify" field). DEFAULT to passes:false unless the evidence is clear. Generic or merely 'fine' work does NOT pass.

STEP 5 — WRITE your verdict to /sandbox/work/verdict.json as STRICT JSON with EXACTLY this shape: {"criteria": [{"id": "c1", "passes": <bool>, "feedback": "concrete browser evidence"}, ...], "allPass": <bool — true ONLY if EVERY criterion passes>, "score": <0-100>, "meets_criteria": <same boolean as allPass>, "feedback": "overall summary of what still fails"}. ALSO update /sandbox/work/contract.json, setting each criterion's "passes" flag to match your verdict. verdict.json is what the loop reads. After writing it, stop the server (pkill -f vite 2>/dev/null; pkill -f 'npm run' 2>/dev/null) and end with a one-line summary.

body.prompt: ${ "Grade the redesigned app in /sandbox/work/repo against EACH criterion in /sandbox/work/contract.json by VIEWING + interacting with it in the browser (Playwright MCP), then WRITE /sandbox/work/verdict.json (per-criterion passes + allPass + meets_criteria) and update contract.json passes flags. Follow your instructions exactly." }

with.command:
for i in $(seq 1 40); do [ -s /sandbox/work/verdict.json ] && break; sleep 1; done
[ -f /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png ] || cp "$(ls -1 /sandbox/work/shots/*.png 2>/dev/null | head -1)" /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png 2>/dev/null
python3 - <<'PYZZ'
import json,re
PROG='/sandbox/work/progress.json'
try:
    d=json.load(open('/sandbox/work/verdict.json'))
except Exception:
    print(json.dumps({'meets_criteria':False,'feedback':'verdict.json missing/invalid'}));raise SystemExit
crit=d.get('criteria') or []
total=len(crit); passed=sum(1 for c in crit if isinstance(c,dict) and c.get('passes'))
allp=d.get('allPass'); mc=d.get('meets_criteria')
txt=(str(d.get('overall_verdict') or d.get('verdict') or '')+' '+str(d.get('summary') or d.get('feedback') or '')).lower()
failw=bool(re.search(r'\b(fail|fails|failed|does not pass|doesn.t pass|not pass|reject\w*)\b',txt))
passw=bool(re.search(r'\b(pass|passes|passed|approved?|meets|satisf\w*|accept\w*)\b',txt))
sc=d.get('score'); scpass=isinstance(sc,(int,float)) and ((sc>=80) or (sc<=10 and sc>=8))
if allp is not None: res=bool(allp)
elif total>0: res=(passed==total)
elif mc is not None: res=bool(mc)
elif failw: res=False
elif passw: res=True
else: res=scpass
try:
    p=json.load(open(PROG))
except Exception:
    p={'baseline':'unknown','log':[]}
p.setdefault('log',[]).append({'iteration':len(p.get('log',[])),'criteriaPassed':passed,'criteriaTotal':total,'allPass':res,'score':sc,'note':(d.get('feedback') or d.get('summary') or '')[:200]})
json.dump(p,open(PROG,'w'))
print(json.dumps({'meets_criteria':res,'score':sc,'passed':passed,'total':total,'feedback':(d.get('feedback') or d.get('summary') or '')[:300]}))
PYZZ

cd /sandbox/work/repo
(nohup npm run preview -- --port 4321 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &) || (nohup npm run dev -- --port 4321 --host 127.0.0.1 >/tmp/preview.log 2>&1 &)
for i in $(seq 1 40); do curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:4321/ >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 1; done
PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=/opt/pw-browsers playwright screenshot --browser chromium --full-page --viewport-size 1280,900 http://127.0.0.1:4321/ /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png || echo SHOT_FAILED
pkill -f vite 2>/dev/null; pkill -f 'npm run' 2>/dev/null
ls -la /sandbox/work/critic-shot.png 2>/dev/null || echo NO-SHOT

artifacts.title: Rendered redesign (Playwright critic screenshot)
artifacts.description: Full-page screenshot the critic captured in-browser via the Playwright MCP server.

artifacts.title: Negotiated acceptance contract
artifacts.description: The Generator↔Evaluator negotiated contract.json (testable criteria + per-criterion passes flags).

with.command: cd /sandbox/work/repo && git config user.email agent@workflow-builder.local && git config user.name 'workflow-builder agent' && BR=redesign-$(date +%s) && git checkout -b $BR && git add -A && (git commit -m 'Redesign Nimbus Notes landing page (workflow-builder plan/generate/critic)' || echo nothing-to-commit) && git push https://x-access-token:$GITHUB_TOKEN@github.com/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo.git HEAD:$BR && PR=$(curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github+json' https://api.github.com/repos/PittampalliOrg/sveltekit-landing-demo/pulls -d "{\"title\":\"Redesign landing page (automated)\",\"head\":\"$BR\",\"base\":\"main\",\"body\":\"Automated redesign via the workflow-builder plan/generate/critic loop; a Playwright critic inspected the rendered UI.\"}") && echo "$PR" | python3 -c 'import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print("PR_URL="+str(d.get("html_url") or d.get("message") or d))'

set.terminalState: ${ if ( ((.loop.last.gate.result.stdout // .loop.last.gate.stdout // .loop.last.gate.result.output // .loop.last.gate.output // "") | test("OBJECTIVE PASS")) and ((( (((.loop.last.read_verdict.result.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.stdout // .loop.last.read_verdict.result.output // .loop.last.read_verdict.output // "{}") | fromjson?) // {}) as $v | ( ($v.meets_criteria) // (($v.overall_verdict // $v.verdict // "") | ascii_downcase | test("pass|approv|meet|satisf|accept")) // (($v.score // 0) >= 80) ) )) == true) ) then "satisfied" else "max_iterations_reached" end }

artifacts.title: Coding redesign result
artifacts.description: Build + critic verdicts, iterations, PR output.
